research_metadata.primary_source: Frank Lotto, Fayette County: Her History and Her People (Schulenburg: Sticker Steam Press, 1902; rpt. Austin: University of Texas Press, 1981) — digitized at https://archive.org/details/fayettecountyher00lott
secondary_sources: Texas State Historical Association Handbook of Texas Online — 'Teichmueller, Hans' by Rudolph L. Biesele (1952, updated 2019): https://www.tshaonline.org/handbook/entries/teichmueller-hans

Basel University archived page on Gustav Teichmüller (2011): https://web.archive.org/web/20111127170425/http://www.unigeschichte.unibas.ch:80/fakultaeten-und-faecher/phil.hist.-fakultaet/zur-geschichte-der-phil.hist.-fakultaet/gustav-teichmueller.html
FamilySearch — Texas Deaths 1890-1976, Minette T Pohl: https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:K36Z-VYY
FamilySearch — Texas Deaths 1890-1976, Annie Elizabeth Teichmueller: https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:K34B-HSQ
Find A Grave — Hans Teichmueller (La Grange Cemetery): https://familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:1:QVP9-MRD2
Find A Grave — Mary Teichmueller Mattingly (Memorial #57057870)
Hans Teichmueller, Biographical Sketch (Austin: Von Boeckmann-Jones, 1908) — cited in TSHA but not yet digitized online

birth.place: Brunswick (Braunschweig), Duchy of Brunswick, German Confederation

death.place: La Grange, Fayette County, Texas, USA

hans_teichmueller_full_profile.burial: La Grange Cemetery, La Grange, Fayette County, Texas
hans_teichmueller_full_profile.birth_order: Third son (TSHA: 'the third son of an army officer')
education: Gymnasium of Blankenburg (in the Harz Mountains, now Saxony-Anhalt, ~40 km southeast of Brunswick) — graduated

father.name: August Teichmüller
father.occupation: Officer in the Brunswick army (Duchy of Brunswick military; Lotto 1902 says 'officer in the Brunswick army'; English Wikipedia for son Gustav says 'lieutenant in the Prussian army' — the Lotto 1902 is the nearer primary source; Basel Univ. archive says 'pensionierter Soldat' / retired soldier)
father.death_note: Died before 1856, causing financial hardship that prompted both sons (Gustav and Hans) to abandon planned university careers. Gustav turned to tutoring; Hans emigrated to Texas.
father.confidence: CONFIRMED — named in Lotto 1902 (primary), confirmed by Wikipedia article on son Gustav Teichmüller

mother.name: Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth Teichmüller, née von Girsewaldt / von Girsewald / von Gursewald
mother.family_background: From a Braunschweig officer/noble family. The von Girsewald family are documented Braunschweig nobles: Freiherren elevated 1827. Notable members include Generalmajor Gustav von Girsewald (1785-1864) and his sons Generalmajor Gustav (1812-1871) and Generalmajor Alexander (1815-1890). Charlotte's exact relationship within the von Girsewald family is unconfirmed in accessible online sources — she is not listed as a daughter of either documented Generalmajor. She may be from a different branch or a sister-line not covered in Wikipedia articles.
mother.english_wiki_note: English Wikipedia (Gustav Teichmüller article) names her 'Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth Teichmüller, née von Girsewaldt' — this detail traces to the now-404 Basel University source (2011 Wayback snapshot says only 'seine Mutter stammte aus einer Offiziersfamilie' / 'his mother came from an officer's family').
mother.confidence: PROBABLE — maiden name von Girsewald confirmed by two independent Wikipedia articles; full forenames 'Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth' from English Wikipedia citing Basel source; the specific branch of the von Girsewald family is UNVERIFIED

siblings.name: Gustav Teichmüller
siblings.occupation: Professor of Philosophy; taught at University of Basel (1868-1871) and Imperial University of Dorpat (1871-1888). His works influenced Friedrich Nietzsche's perspectivism.
siblings.relationship: Older brother — born 5 years before Hans; both orphaned by father's death and forced to abandon planned careers
siblings.confidence: CONFIRMED — named in same parental line by both English and German Wikipedia; Lotto 1902 birth narrative matches Gustav's German Wikipedia article exactly

siblings.name: At least one additional older brother (unnamed)
siblings.note: TSHA says Hans was 'the third son' — Gustav is one older brother; at least one more older brother existed between Gustav (b.1832) and Hans (b.1837).
siblings.confidence: POSSIBLE — based solely on TSHA phrasing; no name in any accessible source

emigration.reason: Father August's death left family in financial straits; Hans abandoned plan to study theology and emigrated to Texas
emigration.first_contact_in_Texas: Old man Fordtrau of Industry (Austin County, Texas) — gave him advice about conditions in the country
emigration.cousin_in_Texas: Arthur Meerscheidt, who lived on a farm in Fayette County — Hans came specifically to join him (TSHA). Meerscheidt later became Hans's law partner.
emigration.route: Brunswick → New York (briefly as cabin boy/sailor, age ~14-15, then returned to Germany) → emigrated permanently 1856, landing in Texas via New York or Galveston. First settled at Shelby, then Postoak Point, then High Hill (1861)
emigration.confidence: CONFIRMED — Lotto 1902 and TSHA agree on year and circumstances

spouse.name: Augusta Dorthea Elizabeth Kellner
spouse.birth_place: Germany (WikiTree; the TSHA says she was born at Postoak Point, Austin County, Texas — discrepancy: WikiTree may be recording the father's origin, not Augusta's)
spouse.death_place: Unknown (she was widowed in La Grange from 1901; Texas death record)
spouse.father: Unknown name; described in Lotto 1902 as 'a highly educated teacher of modern languages.' TSHA adds that this teacher taught Hans English when Hans first arrived in Fayette County — the marriage was thus partly a student-teacher family connection.
spouse.marriage_date_note: Lotto 1902 primary text (digitized) clearly reads 'June 10, 1858.' TSHA (1952, Biesele) says 'June 16, 1858' — this appears to be a transcription error in the TSHA article.
spouse.officiant: Colonel Henderson, then justice of the peace
spouse.confidence: CONFIRMED — name, marriage date/place from Lotto 1902 (primary); birth date from WikiTree citing Texas death records

career: 1856-1861: Farmed at Postoak Point / High Hill area, Fayette County — unsuccessful
1861-1866: Taught school during Civil War at New Ulm; read law simultaneously; served briefly as secretary of a militia at Brenham
1866-05-16: Passed bar examination; entered law firm of L. Lindsey (Judge) and B.D. Shropshire (Judge)
1869-1870: County Judge of Fayette County — filled unexpired term of Reinhardt Hillebrandt; deposed by military order in 1870 (Reconstruction era)
1870s: Law partnerships with Major Phelps, then Major Dunn, then Dunn + Meerscheidt
1884-1901: Elected District Judge, 22nd Judicial District of Texas — continuously without opposition; held until death
Legal writing: Two papers before Bar Association of Texas; articles on trusts, economic freedom, constitutional expansion, American jurisprudence, bankruptcy; planned a book on homestead law

children.name: Louisa Teichmueller
children.death_note: Died before age 8; appeared in 1870 census as age 7, then disappeared. Mother's account (per WikiTree) says she died 'shy of her eighth birthday.'
children.confidence: CONFIRMED — FamilySearch 1870 census; WikiTree profile Teichmueller-2

children.name: Mary (May) Teichmueller Mattingly
children.marriage: Edward Mattingly Sr., married 1 Sep 1894, La Grange, Fayette County, Texas
children.marriage_note: Lotto 1902 says she died 'two years ago' (relative to 1902 publication), confirming ~1900 death. Ed Mattingly was assistant cashier of the First National Bank of La Grange.
children_of_marriage: Leroy Teichmueller Mattingly (b. 1898) — named for maternal grandfather Hans
children.confidence: CONFIRMED — Lotto 1902, Find A Grave #57057870, WikiTree Teichmueller-1, multiple census records

children.name: Minette (Minnetta) Teichmueller Pohl
children.marriage_1: E. R. (Earl P.) Richardson, 22 Jan 1913, Bexar County, Texas
children.marriage_2_note: Death certificate surname is 'Pohl' — she later married Hugo Pohl
children.occupation: Muralist; WPA Federal Art Project artist. Known for 'The Law, Texas Rangers' mural at Smithville, Texas post office.
children.confidence: CONFIRMED — Wikipedia, Texas death certificate (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:K36Z-VYY), WikiTree Teichmueller-4

children.name: Anna E. (Annie Elizabeth) Teichmueller
children.later_life: Lived with sister Minette in San Antonio after 1920; died in San Antonio at age 86
children.confidence: CONFIRMED — Texas death certificate (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:K34B-HSQ), WikiTree Teichmueller-5

children.name: Fifth child (unnamed in all accessible sources)
children.note: Lotto 1902 says 'five children' total. Four are named above (Louisa, Mary, Minette, Anna). The fifth is not named in Lotto 1902 biography (which only names the three surviving daughters at time of writing), nor in any WikiTree profile. No further record found.
children.confidence: POSSIBLE — Lotto 1902 states 'five children' without naming all five

hans_teichmueller_full_profile.confidence: CONFIRMED (Hans's Texas life and family); PROBABLE (German parents' identities); POSSIBLE (von Girsewald branch affiliation)

ancestral_chain_germany.occupation: Officer, Duchy of Brunswick army (Lotto 1902: 'officer in the Brunswick army'); described as 'pensionierter Soldat' (retired soldier) in Basel University source
children_known: at least one unnamed son (second son)
ancestral_chain_germany.confidence: CONFIRMED (name, military status, death timing); UNVERIFIED (birth year, death date, rank)

ancestral_chain_germany.full_name: Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth von Girsewaldt (married: Teichmüller)

birth.place: Duchy of Brunswick (presumed — von Girsewald family is documented in Braunschweig)

ancestral_chain_germany.family_background: From a soldier/officer family (Basel source); the von Girsewald family of Braunschweig were elevated to Freiherren (barons) in 1827
sources: Lotto 1902 p.230 (gives maiden name as 'von Gursewald' — OCR artifact)
English Wikipedia — Gustav Teichmüller (gives full name as 'Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth née von Girsewaldt')
Basel University archived page 2011 (says 'seine Mutter stammte aus einer Offiziersfamilie')
ancestral_chain_germany.confidence: PROBABLE (maiden name von Girsewald); POSSIBLE (full forenames Charlotte Georgine Elisabeth — from one Wikipedia article citing a now-404 source)

ancestral_chain_germany.full_name: Unknown — member of the von Girsewald family of Braunschweig
ancestral_chain_germany.note: The von Girsewald disambiguation article (de.Wikipedia) lists three notable members: Gustav (1785-1864), Gustav (1812-1871), and Alexander (1815-1890). Charlotte was born ~1800-1815. The 1785 Gustav's only documented children are sons Gustav (b.1812) and Alexander (b.1815) — no daughters listed. The Gothaisches Genealogisches Taschenbuch der Freiherrlichen Häuser (1921, p.298) covers the full family tree but is not digitized online. There may be a 'Wilhelm von Girsewald' branch — the 1785 Gustav article mentions a brother Wilhelm who joined the 1809 Dörnberg uprising with him.
sources: German Wikipedia Gustav von Girsewald 1785 article
ancestral_chain_germany.confidence: UNVERIFIED — family identified, specific individual within family not confirmed

ancestral_chain_germany.note: No accessible source names August's parents. The Teichmüller surname is documented in Braunschweig area but no pre-August genealogical records were found in accessible digitized archives.

maternal_von_girsewald_family.note: The von Girsewald family are documented Braunschweig nobles. Charlotte's exact position within this family is unconfirmed. Below are the confirmed family members from Wikipedia.

confirmed_members.name: Conrad Alexander Gustav von Girsewald (Freiherr since 1827)
confirmed_members.rank: Generalmajor, Oberstallmeister (Master of the Horse), Kammerherr (Chamberlain) of the Duchy of Brunswick
children: Gustav Wilhelm Heinrich Ernst von Girsewald (1812-1871)
confirmed_members.note: Fought in the Napoleonic Wars with the Schwarze Schar (Black Corps) of Duke Friedrich Wilhelm of Brunswick. Elevated to Freiherr (baron) 1827.

confirmed_members.name: Gustav Wilhelm Heinrich Ernst Freiherr von Girsewald
confirmed_members.birth: 1812-12-20, York, Ireland (born during family's British service)
children: Katharina (1858-1922, married Generalleutnant Hans von Pawel 1879)
confirmed_members.note: Charlotte von Bülow's daughters were born 1845 and 1858 — too young to be Hans Teichmüller's mother

confirmed_members.name: Alexander von Girsewald
confirmed_members.note: Younger brother of 1812 Gustav; no Wikipedia article exists for him
sources: Mentioned in 1785 Gustav and 1812 Gustav Wikipedia articles

confirmed_members.name: Wilhelm von Girsewald
confirmed_members.note: Brother of the 1785 Gustav (mentioned in Dörnberg uprising). Appears in Braunschweigisches Biographisches Lexikon (vol. I, p.211). No Wikipedia article found. This branch may be Charlotte's line.

siblings_with_descendants.name: Gustav Teichmüller
siblings_with_descendants.fate: Distinguished philosopher; Professor at Basel (1868-1871) and Dorpat (1871-1888). His work influenced Nietzsche. Died of stomach cancer.
marriages: Anna Cramer (daughter of Estonian landowner), married 1858; she died ~1862 after birth of second daughter
Lina Cramer (Anna's sister, his sister-in-law), second marriage; had 8 more children with her
children: Anna Teichmüller (b.1861, first wife Anna Cramer)
Lina Teichmüller (b.~1862, first wife; mother died at birth)
8 more children with Lina Cramer (names not documented in accessible online sources)

siblings_with_descendants.name: Second son (Hans's unnamed older brother)
siblings_with_descendants.note: TSHA says Hans was 'the third son' — a second son between Gustav (b.1832) and Hans (b.1837) is implied but unnamed in all accessible sources.

kellner_family.note: Augusta Kellner's family is documented only as 'a highly educated teacher of modern languages' at Postoak Point, Austin County, Texas. He taught Hans English when Hans first arrived. No first name for the father is given in any accessible source. WikiTree records Augusta's birthplace as 'Germany' suggesting the Kellner family also emigrated from Germany. The connection between the Kellner language teacher and Hans was educational before it was familial.

augusta_profile.birth_place: Germany (WikiTree); or Postoak Point, Austin County, Texas (TSHA implies Texas-born)
augusta_profile.father_occupation: Highly educated teacher of modern languages, Postoak Point, Austin County, Texas
augusta_profile.note_on_birthplace_discrepancy: WikiTree says 'Germany' for birthplace; TSHA implies she was born at Postoak Point. If born 25 Mar 1843 in Texas, the family emigrated before 1843. If born in Germany, they emigrated when Augusta was a young child. Either is plausible for German Texans of that era.

emigration_context.timing: Hans emigrated in 1856, after his father August's death. This is post-1848 but the 1902 book does not frame Hans as a political refugee — the cause was economic (father's death, family poverty).
emigration_context.brunswick_to_texas_corridor: The Duchy of Brunswick was a significant source of Texas German immigrants. The Adelsverein (Society for Protection of German Immigrants in Texas) 1844-1853 focused on central Texas counties including Fayette and Austin counties — exactly where Hans landed. By 1856 the Adelsverein had dissolved but the German community in these counties was well established.
emigration_context.first_contact: Hans's first contact in Texas was 'old man Fordtrau of Industry' — Industry (Austin County) was one of Texas's earliest German settlements, founded by Friedrich Ernst in 1831.
emigration_context.cousin_connection: Arthur Meerscheidt in Fayette County was Hans's cousin — this is a family chain migration pattern, not a political exodus.
emigration_context.naturalization_note: Hans must have naturalized to serve as county judge (1869) and district judge (1884-1901). Fayette County or Travis County naturalization records would confirm year, but were not accessed. The 1900 census record (Hans is enumerated as head of household at La Grange) would record his immigration year, but the FamilySearch page is blocked. WikiTree cites the 1900 census as: FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:M3GQ-SRC.

open_questions: Exact birth and death dates/place for August Teichmüller — NOT in any accessible digitized source; Niedersächsisches Landesarchiv (Braunschweig) holds civil and church records

Charlotte von Girsewald's exact parentage — which branch of the von Girsewald family? The Gothaisches Genealogisches Taschenbuch der Freiherrlichen Häuser (1921, p.298) covers the Girsewald Freiherren but is not digitized; requires archive access
The 'second son' between Gustav (1832) and Hans (1837) — unnamed in all sources; TSHA says Hans was 'the third son'
Identity of the fifth Teichmueller child in Texas (Lotto 1902 says 'five children'; only four are named in accessible records)
Augusta Kellner's father's full name — unnamed in all sources; Austin County Texas records from the 1850s-1860s may help
Hans's immigration year recorded in 1900 census — FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:M3GQ-SRC (blocked but accessible with account)
Naturalization record for Hans — would confirm exact year and county

The 1908 biographical sketch 'Hans Teichmueller, Biographical Sketch' (Austin: Von Boeckmann-Jones, 1908) — cited in TSHA bibliography; not digitized online; UT Austin or Texas State Library may hold a copy
Kellner family origins in Germany — if Augusta was born in Germany (WikiTree), their emigration records would be in German sources

maternal_deepest_confirmed.name: Conrad Alexander Gustav von Girsewald (Freiherr)
maternal_deepest_confirmed.generation: Likely Hans's maternal great-uncle or in-law generation; exact relationship to Charlotte unconfirmed

maternal_deepest_possible_for_charlotte.name: Wilhelm von Girsewald (brother of the 1785 Gustav)
maternal_deepest_possible_for_charlotte.generation: Charlotte's probable father if from the Wilhelm branch
